RETURN.description: Computes exp(x), function result is nearly rounded.   It will be correctly rounded to the theoretical value for 99.9% of input values, otherwise it will have a 1 ULP error.
RETURN.content: Computes exp(x), function result is nearly rounded.   It will be correctly rounded to the theoretical value for 99.9% of input values, otherwise it will have a 1 ULP error.

PARAM.description: Base of the logarithm, must be greater than 0.
PARAM.name: base
PARAM.content: Base of the logarithm, must be greater than 0.

RETURN.description: Returns NaN if either argument is negative. If base is 0 and x is positive, 0 is returned. If base is positive and x is 0, Double.NEGATIVE_INFINITY is returned. If both arguments are 0, the result is NaN.
RETURN.assertion: assert methodResultID == (args[0] <= 0 || args[1] <= 0) ? Double.isNaN(methodResultID) : ((args[0] == 0 && args[1] > 0) ? 0 : ((args[0] > 0 && args[1] == 0) ? Double.NEGATIVE_INFINITY : (args[0] == 0 && args[1] == 0) ? Double.isNaN(methodResultID) : methodResultID));
RETURN.content: Returns NaN if either argument is negative. If base is 0 and x is positive, 0 is returned. If base is positive and x is 0, Double.NEGATIVE_INFINITY is returned. If both arguments are 0, the result is NaN.

RETURN.description: phase angle of point (x,y) between -PI and PI
RETURN.content: phase angle of point (x,y) between -PI and PI
